codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly covers all expected behavior requirements for PresetStore. The code follows TDD approach with comprehensive tests (21 test cases, 72.7% coverage). Atomic write pattern, file permissions, JSON formatting, and error handling are all properly implemented. Convenience methods (GetPreset, SetPreset, DeletePreset, SetActivePreset, ListPresets) provide good API ergonomics.

issues.description: Documentation discrepancy: architecture.md defines Parameters as `map[string]*ParamDef` but implementation uses `[]ParamDef` with Name field. Both approaches are valid - slice preserves parameter order and is more JSON-friendly. The validation contract VAL-STORAGE-004 references 'parameters array' suggesting slice is correct intent. Recommend updating architecture.md to match implementation.

ParamDef expected behavior listed only 'type, required, default, description, pattern' but implementation includes 'name' field. This is actually better than required as parameters need names. Feature description could be clarified to include 'name' explicitly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used `[]ParamDef` (slice) for Parameters field instead of `map[string]*ParamDef` documented in architecture.md. Both are valid design choices - slice preserves order and includes Name field directly. The validation contract VAL-STORAGE-004 references 'parameters array' which aligns with the implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: store.go:20 shows `Parameters []ParamDef`, architecture.md:35 shows `Parameters map[string]*ParamDef`. Tests validate slice approach works. Validation contract says 'parameters array'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed TDD procedure correctly - tests were written with the implementation. The preset-worker skill procedure was followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true. Git diff shows both store.go and store_test.go added together.

summary: PASS: PresetStore implementation fully meets feature requirements. All 11 expected behaviors verified: NewPresetStore path, Load/Save operations, missing file handling, corrupted JSON error, directory creation, 0600 permissions, atomic write, JSON formatting, QueryPreset/ParamDef structures, and active preset persistence. 21 test cases with 72.7% coverage. Minor documentation discrepancy noted (Parameters slice vs map in architecture.md) but implementation is correct per validation contract.
